public_reputation: Shirsh noble whose authority overlaps with observatory prestige, celestial study, and strategic information.
appearance: Lord Corith carries the marks of lord in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a lord whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Luminary Outpost.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of lord in and around Luminary Outpost, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Luminary Outpost while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Lord Corith can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Luminary Outpost.
Lord Corith becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a lord.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Lord Corith is readable as a lord tied to Luminary Outpost.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Luminary Outpost: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Lord Corith presents as someone shaped by the duties of a lord and the daily pressures of Luminary Outpost.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a lord, and proof that strangers will not make Luminary Outpost harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Lord Corith refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a lord too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Lord Corith can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Lord Corith adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Lord Corith always knows more about Luminary Outpost than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to lord duties in Luminary Outpost.
social_texture: Lord Corith speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Luminary Outpost, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Lord touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Lord Corith when the party needs a socially grounded face for Luminary Outpost: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
